title: cratoxylum neriifolium
intro: Cratoxylum neriifolium, also known as the Philippine oak or Philippine forest oak, is a plant species native to the Philippines. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cratoxylum neriifolium include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Cratoxylum neriifolium are traditionally used in folk medicine, often in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential therapeutic applications.
